core_mechanism: spectral zeta-function regularization: assigning a finite value to the divergent sum of eigenmode energies of a self-adjoint boundary-value operator (with spatially varying coefficient) via analytic continuation, then subtracting a reference spectrum to renormalize
brief_reason: The abstract obtains exact normal modes by solving a Klein-Gordon eigenvalue problem with a position-dependent effective mass and evaluates the vacuum (zero-point) energy through generalized zeta-function regularization plus renormalization, which is the core transferable machinery.
